assertions.text: domain-aggregate-subfolders: domain/article/article.ts and domain/tip/tip.ts (not flat in domain/)
assertions.evidence: domain/article/article.ts and domain/tip/tip.ts confirmed in the directory structure
use-cases/article/{create-article.ts, get-article.ts, publish-article.ts} and use-cases/tip/{create-tip.ts, get-tip.ts}

assertions.text: ports-per-aggregate: each aggregate has its own port files in application/ports/outbound/database/article/ and /tip/
assertions.evidence: application/ports/outbound/database/article/{database-read.ts, database-write.ts} and /tip/{database-read.ts, database-write.ts}; cache ports also per-aggregate

assertions.text: messaging-only-for-article: Kafka/messaging port and adapter exist only for Article, not for Tip
assertions.evidence: application/ports/outbound/messaging/article/messaging.ts only; adapters/outbound/messaging/kafka/article-event-bus.ts only; no messaging port or adapter for Tip

assertions.text: single-bootstrap-wires-both: single bootstrap.ts mounts both ArticleController and TipController on the same Express app
assertions.evidence: adapters/inbound/http/express/bootstrap.ts mounts app.use('/articles', ...) and app.use('/tips', ...); single composition root

assertions.text: separate-controllers: separate controller files for Article and Tip
shared-infrastructure-connections: one Postgres/MongoDB/Redis instance shared, not duplicated per aggregate
assertions.evidence: infrastructure/ has single postgres/connection.ts, mongodb/connection.ts, redis/connection.ts — wired once in bootstrap and passed to both aggregate's adapters
